subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Heat Cramps and Hyponatremia
description: A short lesson to help you apply Heat Cramps and Hyponatremia.

hook.text: A construction worker chugs water all day. By afternoon he's dizzy and confused—from drinking too much.

buildup.text: Heat cramps result from salt loss through sweating. The muscles seize without enough sodium.

discovery.text: But drinking only water without replacing salt dilutes blood sodium—causing hyponatremia.

twist.text: Hyponatremia can cause seizures and death. Marathon runners have died from overhydration.

climax.text: Electrolyte drinks or salty snacks with water maintain the sodium balance during heavy sweating.

punchline.text: Water isn't enough. Your body loses salt too. Replace both or pay.

quiz.question: What is hyponatremia?
options: Dangerously low blood sodium from drinking too much water without replacing salt
